fields.description:
## Why
A significant epic failure showed leaf plans DRIFT as leaf tickets are claimed and edited: by epic close, the leaf ACs described a very different plan than what the epic said would be delivered, and nothing caught the divergence until the epic's completion verification. No current criterion checks a leaf plan against its PARENT'S plan — G1G2/E4 ground against the codebase, COH checks internal coherence. IMPORTANT CORRECTION (verified against the code): there is NO existing "ancestor carve-out" that injects a parent's PLAN TEXT — `pass1._ticket_graph_blob` (pass1.py:281) injects only the parent's ticket ID string into context. So injecting the parent's actual plan text is NEW work this ticket must build, not a mechanism to mirror. The review that gates the CLAIM is the right moment to catch drift, ticket by ticket, instead of at epic close.

## What
- Add a new plan-review criterion (working id `G7`, facet parent-containment) to src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json + the registry: `exec: AGENT` (it must read the parent ticket from the store, like the other codebase-grounded criteria), with a rubric prompt at src/rebar/llm/reviewers/ following the existing G-series prompt conventions.
- ROUTING — fires ONLY on leaf tickets WITH a parent: the gate is (ticket has a parent_id) AND (ticket has no children). MECHANISM (the existing schema can't express "has_parent_id" — `applies()` in registry.py:311-339 gates only on `scope` (container/leaf), `suppress_types`, `suppress_when`): add a new boolean axis `require_parent_id` to the `applies_at` schema and to `applies()`, and thread the ticket's `has_parent_id` fact through `route_criteria()` (orchestrator.py:337-375, which calls `registry.applies()`) from the assembled PlanContext (which already knows the parent chain). So G7's routing = `applies_at: {scope: ["leaf"], require_parent_id: true, suppress_types: ["bug","session_log"]}`. The `scope:["leaf"]` gives "no children"; `require_parent_id` gives "has a parent"; bugs/session_logs stay exempt as today; parentless tickets and containers never run it. `validate_packaged_routing()` and the applies_at schema validator must accept the new axis.
- SEMANTICS: the leaf's What/Scope/ACs must be a SUBSET of the parent's declared scope — a finding fires when the leaf (a) delivers something the parent's plan does not contain or implies is out of scope, (b) contradicts a parent AC/success criterion, or (c) redefines a deliverable the parent specifies differently. Consistent narrowing (a leaf doing PART of the parent) is NOT a finding.
- CONFLICT RULE (the operator's directive, encoded in the rubric AND the Pass-4 coaching move): the PARENT WINS — it holds the full delivery context. Coaching directs the agent to realign the leaf to the parent; if the agent believes the parent is genuinely wrong, the productive move is to update the PARENT first (which stales the parent's own attestation and forces its re-review) and only then re-review the leaf — never to silently diverge the leaf.
- CONTEXT ASSEMBLY (NEW mechanism — named edit targets): extend the plan-review context so the criterion receives the parent's title + description (and the grandparent's, one level up, if present), BOUNDED (truncate each to a char cap; no whole-subtree dumps). Concretely: add a `parent_plan` field (parent + grandparent title/description, bounded) to `det_floor.PlanContext` (det_floor.py:95); populate it in `orchestrator._assemble_context_uncached` (orchestrator.py:163) by reading the parent chain from the store. SURFACING (precise seam, verified against the code): G7 runs in the verify pass, whose context is emitted by `plan_review_verify_inputs` (workflow_ops.py:250) as `plan = assemble_context(tid).plan_text` and rendered into the verifier prompt's `{{plan}}` slot. G7 additionally needs the PARENT's plan text, so: (1) `plan_review_verify_inputs` emits a NEW `parent_plan` value from `PlanContext.parent_plan`; (2) the plan-review verifier prompt gains a `{{parent_plan}}` slot that G7's rubric reads. NAMING NOTE: pass1.py:118 already binds a prompt var literally named `parent_plan` to `ctx.plan_text` (the ticket's OWN plan, in the finder path) — to avoid collision, the verify-side field carrying the actual parent's plan is named `ancestor_plan` (or the pass1 var is left untouched and only the verify path gains the new slot). Bounded via a CHAR CAP of 2000 chars per ancestor ticket (parent + one grandparent → ≤ ~4000 chars), truncated with an ellipsis marker; `det_floor.est_tokens` is used to keep it within budget. This is additive — criteria that don't read the new slot are unaffected; `pass1._ticket_graph_blob` (the finder's parent-ID string) is NOT the seam and is left unchanged.
- Pass-2/3 stay untouched: findings flow through the standard verify -> decide pipeline; severity maps onto the existing plan axes (divergent_implementation is the natural home — a leaf diverging from its parent is exactly a divergent-implementation signal).
- POSTURE: ship `default_posture: advisory` at 0.95 per the ADR-0036 expand-contract house pattern, BUT record fire-rate + adjudication from day one. IN-SESSION deliverable (the promotion-decision PATH, not the decision itself), as a COMMITTED REPO ARTIFACT (CI-verifiable that the file exists, not a one-time ticket comment): a short section appended to `docs/plan-review-gate.md` (or a new `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/g7-promotion-path.md`) documenting (a) the exact query to compute G7's fire-rate + validity from the `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecars, (b) the promotion trigger — blocking at the plan-review 0.6–0.7 band once the observation window shows an acceptable false-fire rate — and (c) where the actual promotion decision will be recorded. The actual promotion decision depends on post-deployment field data and is explicitly DEFERRED (out of this ticket's close path; it belongs with the epic's deferred measurement follow-up 72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d).
- NOTE (explicit non-goal here, candidate follow-up): a material PARENT edit does not currently stale the CHILDREN'S plan-review attestations, so drift introduced by editing the epic after leaves are reviewed is only caught on the leaf's next re-review. If the observation window shows parent-edit drift, file a follow-up for parent-edit-propagating staleness in attest.compute_validity.

## Scope
src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json, src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py (register G7 in CANONICAL_LLM/AGENT_TIER/CODEBASE_GROUNDED; add the `require_parent_id` axis to the applies_at schema + `applies()`), src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py (`route_criteria()` threads the ticket's has_parent_id fact into `applies()`), a new reviewers/plan_review_G7.md rubric prompt, the coach MOVE_REGISTRY (one parent-realign move in passes.py), the context-assembly path for the NEW parent-plan injection — det_floor.py `PlanContext` (add `parent_plan` field), orchestrator.py `_assemble_context_uncached` (populate it from the parent chain), plan_review/workflow_ops.py (`plan_review_verify_inputs` emits the ancestor_plan value from PlanContext; `route_criteria` threads has_parent_id), the plan-review verifier prompt(s) (new {{parent_plan}}/ancestor_plan slot G7 reads) — the regenerated docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md, and tests (routing: fires on leaf-with-parent only, skipped for parentless/container/bug/session_log; semantics: fixture parent/leaf pairs — contained/exceeding/contradicting; a test asserting parent_plan context assembly; posture pin; validate_packaged_routing()==[]). No kernel decide.py/verify.py math changes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The criterion runs ONLY on tickets that have a parent_id and no children; a routing test proves it is skipped for parentless tickets, containers, bugs, and session_logs.
- [ ] Fixture-driven tests: a leaf narrowing its parent PASSES; a leaf adding out-of-parent-scope deliverables fires; a leaf contradicting a parent AC fires.
- [ ] The rubric and the Pass-4 coaching move state the parent-wins rule and direct remediation toward realigning the leaf or updating the parent FIRST — never silently diverging the leaf.
- [ ] The criterion ships advisory (0.95) with fire-rate observable in REVIEW_RESULT sidecars. In-session proof (a COMMITTED repo file, CI-verifiable): a docs section (docs/plan-review-gate.md or docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/g7-promotion-path.md) documents the promotion-decision PATH — the fire-rate/validity query, the 0.6–0.7 promotion band + trigger, and where the decision is recorded. The actual promotion decision is explicitly deferred (see 72b6), not required to close this ticket.
- [ ] The parent's plan text is injected into the criterion context (bounded to parent + grandparent), with a test asserting the assembly.
- [ ] make lint passes on the change.
- [ ] make typecheck passes on the change.
- [ ] make test passes on the change.
